message:
fix(process,signal): non-destructive Child::wait with backoff, signal recv returns None instead of hanging

process.rs: Rewrite `Child::wait()` from a blocking `child.wait()` that
consumed the inner handle via `take()` (making the Child permanently unusable)
to a cooperative spin-then-backoff loop using `try_wait()`. First 32 iterations
use `spin_loop()` + `yield_now()` for low-latency wakeup; subsequent iterations
fall back to 1ms sleeps. Crucially, the process handle is no longer consumed,
so the Child remains valid after wait() returns. Dead commented-out code for
nonexistent self.code/self.signal fields removed.

signal.rs: Change `Signal::recv()` from `std::future::pending().await` (which
would hang forever, silently deadlocking any select! branch awaiting a signal)
to immediately returning `None`. This is the correct sentinel for "signal
subsystem not yet implemented" -- callers see an exhausted stream and can handle
the absent-signal case instead of hanging indefinitely. Added
`#[allow(clippy::unused_async)]` since the method retains its async signature
for API stability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
